edge: Neural distance functions trained via volumetric ray marching have been widely adopted for multi-view 3D reconstruction.
The ray marching procedure is applied for the entire scene volume.
Applying the ray marching procedure for the entire scene volume leads to reduced sampling efficiency.
The new coarse sphere-based surface reconstruction is used to exclude the empty volume of the scene from the volumetric ray marching procedure.
Joint training of the implicit function and the new coarse sphere-based surface reconstruction is used to address the reduced sampling efficiency issue.
The implicit function in joint training is used to exclude the empty volume of the scene.
Excluding the empty volume without additional forward passes of the neural surface network leads to increased fidelity of the reconstructions.
The approach is evaluated by incorporating it into the training procedures of implicit surface modeling methods.
By incorporating the approach into the training procedures of implicit surface modeling methods, uniform improvements are observed across both synthetic and real-world datasets.

node_2: modern computer vision and graphics research
edge: The task of multi-view 3D reconstruction remains the focus of modern computer vision and graphics research.
Multi-view 3D reconstruction has major practical significance in AR/VR metaverses, synthetic media, medical imaging, and the special effects industry.
MVS reconstruction systems estimate the underlying scene geometry.
MVS reconstruction systems estimate scene geometry in the form of a point cloud using photometric consistency between different views.
Recent methods have largely phased out stereo reconstruction systems in favor of representing scenes as neural implicit fields.
Approaches using neural implicit fields have multiple advantages compared to classical MVS.
Neural implicit fields can easily accommodate non-Lambertian and texture-less surfaces.
Neural implicit fields can achieve an impressive quality of renders.

node_2: methods specialized in opaque surface reconstruction
edge: This work focuses on improving methods specialized in opaque surface reconstruction.

node_1: methods specialized in opaque surface reconstruction
edge: Approaches for opaque surface reconstruction often employ neural signed distance fields.
Neural signed distance fields are often trained using volumetric ray marching.
The sampling efficiency during the training step largely determines the resulting quality of reconstructions.
The training step involves stochastic elements such as sampling rays corresponding to training pixels and sampling color integral points along the rays.
The point sampling procedure typically employs a multi-stage importance sampling method.
The point sampling procedure typically employs uncertainty sampling in the abovementioned methods.
The point sampling procedure typically employs a multi-stage importance sampling to improve the accuracy of the reconstructions.
The point sampling procedure typically employs a multi-stage uncertainty sampling to improve the accuracy of the reconstructions.
Neural signed distance fields benefit from the surface-based sampling of rays for surface rendering methods, such as IDR.

The novel-view synthesis works successfully combined a simple two-stage coarse-to-fine sampling with explicitly defined surface guides to achieve a better rendering quality, as opposed to using the sophisticated multi-stage sampling procedures of the surface reconstruction methods.
To guide the ray marching, they use explicit coarse surface approximations in the form of a set of volumetric primitives or sparse octrees.
We improve the existing surface reconstruction methods’ ray sampling and marching procedures using explicitly defined coarse representations.
We propose training a coarse reconstruction as a sphere cloud which guides both sampling steps during volume rendering.
We also propose a new optimization approach for coarse reconstruction.
Gradient descent is proposed as a new optimization approach for coarse reconstruction based on volume rendering.
The new optimization approach of gradient descent allows training alongside the implicit surface field.
Point resampling scheme prevents the spheres from getting stuck in local minima.
Spheres are prevented from getting stuck in the local minima due to the point resampling scheme.
The repulsion mechanism ensures high degrees of exploration of the reconstructed surface.
The proposed method is applicable to different approaches for implicit surface modeling.
The proposed method is paired with several modern systems for surface reconstruction.
The proposed method is paired with modern systems for surface reconstruction to observe improvements in quality across 3D benchmarks.
Neural implicit representations have gained attention for multi-view 3D surface reconstruction.
Training approaches are based on the differentiable rendering of implicit functions for multi-view 3D surface reconstruction.
Surface rendering approximates the pixel's color using the radiance of a single point in the volume.
Training procedures are based on volume rendering with multiple samples via ray marching.
Neural Radiance Fields were introduced in the novel view synthesis work.
Neural Radiance Fields introduced volumetric ray marching in the context of novel view synthesis, which later led to its adaptation to surface modeling for improved reconstruction quality.
The ray marching procedure estimates the color along the ray using the volume rendering integral, approximated as a sum of weighted radiances at multiple points throughout the volume.

Works mentioned in the context employ methods based on surface intersection-based sampling, uncertainty, and importance for obtaining points used in ray marching, leading to increased approximation accuracy.

The proposed hybrid surface representation improves ray marching by limiting the sampling space to a volume coarsely bounding the scene, optimizing the selection of samples around the reconstructed surface.
The hybrid surface representation is used to guide the sampling of training rays, ultimately improving the quality of reconstructions given the same training time.
